response_constraints: Base the final answer only on returned tool results; do not imply that a skipped or failed call succeeded.
Respect the stage boundaries: calls grouped as parallel must be issued together in one assistant action, and later calls must wait for the IDs, status, date, or mutation result they consume.
For any requested field absent from a tool result, write "unknown" instead of inferring a value.
Do not use extra write or notification tools beyond the single action path explicitly requested.

stages.purpose: Resolve the two independent names in one parallel search action.
Retrieve the unique stage-1 matches together before checking the mutation condition.
Cancel Chicago volunteer summit only after stage 2 verifies its unique ID and draft status.

arguments.body: Chicago volunteer summit was cancelled after status verification.
arguments.subject: Record update: Chicago volunteer summit

stages.purpose: Notify the named operational recipient only after stage 3 returns a successful mutation.

state_assertions: Record tra-682 changes from draft to cancelled with the stated reason.
Record tra-282 and distractor tra-1082 remain unchanged.
Exactly one notification is sent to travel coordinator, and only after the mutation succeeds.

prompt: Resolve Reykjavík research trip in Reykjavík and Chicago volunteer summit in Chicago with parallel searches, then retrieve both unique matches together. If Chicago volunteer summit is still draft, cancel only Chicago volunteer summit, with reason "Traveler chose a different itinerary". Leave Reykjavík research trip unchanged. Only after that mutation succeeds, notify travel coordinator with the outcome; do not send a notice if the mutation is skipped or fails. Report only returned fields, and label any missing requested detail as unknown. Do not take any extra write or notification action beyond the path I specified.

stage_4.body: Chicago volunteer summit was cancelled after status verification.
